summary: Deterministic command registry, doctor status, skill disclosure, product-import isolation, and Cautilus Agent flow audit tests prove the binary/Cautilus Agent ownership boundary.

covered: The Cautilus Agent owns sequencing, decision boundaries, review-budget explanation, and claim curation flow behavior.
The binary owns deterministic command discovery and packet surfaces without importing LLM provider SDKs or embedding provider API hosts in product code.
Progressive disclosure between source and packaged Cautilus Agent copies is checked deterministically, including required command-discovery fragments and forbidden stale or split-surface guidance.
notClaimed: This bundle does not prove live LLM reviewer quality.
This bundle does not claim subagents were spawned in this session; it proves the checked-in boundary and audit contracts.

commandEvidence.command: npm run lint:skill-disclosure && npm run lint:product-import-isolation
outputSummary: source and packaged Cautilus Agent skills byte-identical under the line cap
product import isolation status ok with only gopkg.in/yaml.v3 as allowed third-party import
forbidden provider hosts checked: api.anthropic.com, api.openai.com, generativelanguage.googleapis.com, api.x.ai
product-import isolation self-tests reject LLM provider SDK imports and provider host strings
first-scan audit requires doctor status, scan-scope confirmation, and separate LLM review budget wording
review-prepare audit stops at deterministic packet creation
reviewer-launch audit requires selected review budget and a cautilus.claim_review_result.v1 packet before completion
claim-discovery curation audit requires extraction, duplicate, false-positive, false-negative, and bucket curation without overrunning into eval or review work

checkedInEvidence.proves: Progressive disclosure is deterministic across the source and packaged Cautilus Agent copies, including required command-discovery fragments and forbidden duplicated operational guidance.
The binary product code is statically checked against direct LLM provider SDK imports and provider API host strings.
First-scan skill behavior starts from doctor status, asks for scan-scope confirmation, keeps LLM review budget separate, and stops before review or eval overrun.
Review preparation remains deterministic packet creation and does not launch or apply reviewer results.
Reviewer launch is a budgeted branch whose completion boundary is a cautilus.claim_review_result.v1 packet, not an unbounded chat claim.
Cautilus Agent owns claim curation behavior after discovery, including false-positive and false-negative review boundaries.
The binary owns deterministic command discovery, help text, examples, and command grouping.
The binary exposes command registry JSON and a read-only doctor status orientation packet for the Cautilus Agent.

decision.reason: The checked-in scanners and tests exercise the binary's deterministic command/import boundary and the Cautilus Agent's sequencing, review-budget, claim-curation, and progressive-disclosure responsibilities.
